name: datamol
tagline: Reference skill teaching Claude to use datamol (a Pythonic RDKit wrapper) for cheminformatics: SMILES, descriptors, clustering, conformers.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped library-usage guide. No credential harvesting, no piped remote-code execution, no hidden instructions; cloud I/O is explicitly gated behind user confirmation and only reads the standard provider env vars locally. Only real nits: the file runs ~723 lines (over the ~500-line hygiene budget) and one code snippet's step numbering skips '#2'. S

highlights.summary: Minor inconsistency: the 'Virtual Screening Pipeline' snippet numbers comments 1, 3, 4, 5 and skips '2' (lines 591-608) — cosmetic, not functionally confusing.
File is ~723 lines; deep API detail is delegated to 6 external reference files (references/*.md) but the SKILL.md body itself is still long for a single load.
Document is ~723 lines, roughly 45% over the ~500-line hygiene budget, despite offloading deep detail to external reference files.
